chief_complaint: A 71-year-old male presented with myalgia, anorexia, and anuria, along with intermittent chest pain for 1 month.
history: The patient has a history of diabetes, chronic kidney disease (CKD) stage G3aA3, and chronic myelogenous leukemia. He reported progressive symptoms including decreased urine output and anuria.
exam: No specific physical examination findings are provided.
labs: Laboratory findings revealed acute kidney injury (serum creatinine: 12.14 mg/dL), extreme elevations in muscle enzymes (creatine kinase: 250,000 IU/L), and cardiac biomarkers (troponin-T: 543 ng/L). Thyroid function tests confirmed non-autoimmune hypothyroidism (TSH: 55.58 muIU/mL, free T4: 0.06 ng/dL).
